title: Pimpinella papillare
intro:
There is limited information available on Pimpinella papillare, however, I'll provide information on a similar plant, Pimpinella anisum, also known as Pimpinella papillare is not found in the literature and is possibly a confusion with Pimpinella anisum (Anise, or Aniseed) which is a flowering plant in the family Apiaceae.

Pimpinella anisum, or Anise, is an annual herb with a sweet, licorice-like flavor and aroma, commonly used as a culinary spice and medicinal plant. The health benefits of Pimpinella anisum include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pimpinella anisum include anethole, a phenolic compound responsible for its medicinal properties, as well as other essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The most common herbal preparations made with Pimpinella anisum include teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as anise oil, which is often used in aromatherapy and traditional medicine.
